page.title: Azimut 39 vs Fairline Targa 12M: Marbella Boat Showdown

data.meta_description: Azimut 39 vs Fairline Targa 12M Marbella: compare specs, capacity, and price. Find your ideal boat charter from Puerto Banús. Book now.
data.summary: A direct comparison of the Azimut 39 and Fairline Targa 12M for charter in Marbella, covering capacity, price, and onboard experience from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Azimut 39: 12.5m, 11 pax, from €749/2h (skipper, drinks, VAT included)
Fairline Targa 12M: 12m, 10 pax, pricing on request via WhatsApp
Both depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella
Standard inclusions: licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, snacks, insurance, IVA
Azimut 39 is a Tier A boat with fixed rates; Fairline Targa 12M is custom-priced
Azimut 39 offers more passenger capacity (11 vs 10) at a lower starting price
Fairline Targa 12M is a sport cruiser with a sportier aesthetic and layout

faq.q: Which boat is bigger, the Azimut 39 or Fairline Targa 12M?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 is 12.5m long, while the Fairline Targa 12M is 12m. Both are similar in size, but the Azimut 39 has a slightly larger beam and more interior space, accommodating up to 11 passengers versus 10 on the Fairline.

faq.q: How much does it cost to charter the Azimut 39 in Marbella?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 is a Tier A boat with fixed pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €1,049 for 3 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,799 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All rates include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.

faq.q: How do I book the Fairline Targa 12M?
faq.a: The Fairline Targa 12M is priced on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote based on your preferred duration, date, and any extras. We'll confirm availability and provide a tailored price.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the Azimut 39 or Fairline Targa 12M?
faq.a: Yes, you're welcome to bring your own food and additional beverages on both boats. The standard charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No corkage fees apply.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a party group?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 can take up to 11 passengers, one more than the Fairline Targa 12M. It also has a larger cockpit and sundeck, making it ideal for socialising. The Fairline Targa 12M offers a sportier feel with a more enclosed cabin, better for smaller, intimate groups.

faq.q: Do both boats include a skipper?
faq.a: Yes, every charter from our fleet includes a licensed skipper. You don't need a boating licence. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety, so you can relax and enjoy the Costa del Sol coastline.

faq.q: Where do the Azimut 39 and Fairline Targa 12M depart from?
faq.a: Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. You'll meet the skipper at the port, and from there you can cruise to Marbella Bay, Cabopino, Estepona, or even Sotogrande depending on your itinerary.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for these charters?
faq.a: Cancellations made at least 48 hours before departure are fully refundable. Within 48 hours, a 50% charge applies. No-shows or same-day cancellations are non-refundable. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Azimut 39 and the Fairline Targa 12M for your Marbella boat charter? Both are premium motor yachts departing from Puerto Banús, but they cater to slightly different tastes and budgets. This head-to-head comparison breaks down their specs, capacity, pricing, and onboard vibe so you can decide which fits your day on the Costa del Sol.

Azimut 39 vs Fairline Targa 12M: At a glance

FeatureAzimut 39Fairline Targa 12M
Length12.5 m12 m
Max passengers1110
Departure portPuerto BanúsPuerto Banús
PricingFrom €749/2h (fixed)On request via WhatsApp
InclusionsSkipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVASkipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVA
StyleFlybridge cruiserSport cruiser

Specs and size: Which boat is bigger?

The Azimut 39 measures 12.5 metres in length, while the Fairline Targa 12M is 12 metres. Both are similar in overall footprint, but the Azimut 39 has a wider beam and a flybridge, offering more deck space and a second helm station upstairs. The Fairline Targa 12M, with its sportier lines, has a lower profile and a more enclosed cockpit, giving it a sleek, aggressive look. If you value open-air lounging and panoramic views, the Azimut 39's flybridge is a clear advantage.

Passenger capacity: Who can come aboard?

The Azimut 39 can carry up to 11 passengers, while the Fairline Targa 12M is licensed for 10. That one extra seat might not sound like much, but for groups of friends or families, it can make the difference between fitting everyone comfortably or leaving someone behind. Both boats have ample sun pads and seating, but the Azimut 39's flybridge adds a separate social zone, perfect for spreading out.

Pricing and value: Fixed vs custom

The Azimut 39 falls under our Tier A pricing, meaning transparent, fixed rates: €749 for 2 hours, €1,049 for 3 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,799 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. The Fairline Targa 12M is priced on request — simply contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. For budget-conscious groups, the Azimut 39 offers clear, upfront value.

Onboard experience and amenities

Both boats come standard with a full drinks package (soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) and light snacks. You can bring your own food and extra drinks at no extra cost. The Azimut 39's flybridge provides a shaded upper deck with a second steering position, ideal for watching the Marbella coastline glide by. The Fairline Targa 12M, with its sporty cockpit and lower helm, feels more like a performance craft — great for those who enjoy the sensation of speed and a more intimate cabin layout.

Destinations from Puerto Banús

Both yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the heart of Marbella's luxury scene. Popular cruising routes include:

  • Marbella Bay and the Golden Mile
  • Cabo Pino and its natural dunes
  • Estepona and the charming old town
  • Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés for swimming
  • Sotogrande for a longer day trip

Your skipper will tailor the itinerary to your preferences, whether you want to anchor for lunch, swim in crystal-clear coves, or simply cruise along the coast with La Concha mountain as your backdrop.

Which boat should you choose?

If you're after fixed pricing, maximum passenger capacity, and a flybridge for socialising, the Azimut 39 is the smart choice. It's our best-value Tier A boat for groups of up to 11. If you prefer a sportier, more exclusive feel and don't mind requesting a custom quote, the Fairline Targa 12M offers a different aesthetic and a slightly more intimate experience. Both deliver the same high standard of service, safety, and inclusions.

Ready to book? Browse our full fleet or contact us on WhatsApp to check availability for either yacht.

Frequently asked questions

Which boat is bigger, the Azimut 39 or Fairline Targa 12M?

The Azimut 39 is 12.5m long, while the Fairline Targa 12M is 12m. Both are similar in size, but the Azimut 39 has a slightly larger beam and more interior space, accommodating up to 11 passengers versus 10 on the Fairline.

How much does it cost to charter the Azimut 39 in Marbella?

The Azimut 39 is a Tier A boat with fixed pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €1,049 for 3 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,799 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All rates include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.

How do I book the Fairline Targa 12M?

The Fairline Targa 12M is priced on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote based on your preferred duration, date, and any extras. We'll confirm availability and provide a tailored price.

Can I bring my own food and drink on the Azimut 39 or Fairline Targa 12M?

Yes, you're welcome to bring your own food and additional beverages on both boats. The standard charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No corkage fees apply.

Which boat is better for a party group?

The Azimut 39 can take up to 11 passengers, one more than the Fairline Targa 12M. It also has a larger cockpit and sundeck, making it ideal for socialising. The Fairline Targa 12M offers a sportier feel with a more enclosed cabin, better for smaller, intimate groups.

Do both boats include a skipper?

Yes, every charter from our fleet includes a licensed skipper. You don't need a boating licence. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety, so you can relax and enjoy the Costa del Sol coastline.

Where do the Azimut 39 and Fairline Targa 12M depart from?

Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. You'll meet the skipper at the port, and from there you can cruise to Marbella Bay, Cabopino, Estepona, or even Sotogrande depending on your itinerary.

What is the cancellation policy for these charters?

Cancellations made at least 48 hours before departure are fully refundable. Within 48 hours, a 50% charge applies. No-shows or same-day cancellations are non-refundable. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.
